dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: Hey everybody, it's Haitch and welcome to the latest installment of Dune Pod, podcast where we talk to our friends about the movies we love. This week, I'm joined as always by my co-host Jason.

dialogues.name: JASON
dialogues.text: I like that. I like that. I like what you're doing with your dirty mind.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH

dialogues.name: IAN
dialogues.text: I bet the runes are just gonna be Kev and Silly standing there shirtless by the doors.

dialogues.name: MATT HAITCH
dialogues.text: We tackle the ultimate A24 horror-gem, featuring Dune: Part Two's Florence Pugh in her star-making performance, Ari Astor's Midsommar, streaming now on Showtime and Kanopy. If you enjoy the show, we need your help. Take a minute to leave us a five star rating and a review wherever you listen to your podcasts or just tell your friends about us because it really does help new listeners find the show. We also have a Discord server where you can hang out with us online whenever you want, a link is in the show notes. And now, without further a-Dune, Midsommar.

Dani is a talented young psychology grad student whose life is reduced to a sunless space when her sister loses her life to suicide, killing their parents along the way. Utterly devastated Dani is completely reliant on her boyfriend Christian, who is emotionally checked out, only staying with her out of guilt and the fear of striking out on his own. But their fates change when they and their friends Josh and Mark accept an invitation from Christian's classmate Pelle to attend a midsummer celebration at his ancestral commune, the Hårga.
